questions.question: What is the difference between problem-focused coping and emotion-focused coping?

options.text: Problem-focused coping is better than emotion-focused coping
Problem-focused coping addresses the stressor directly (changeable stressors); emotion-focused coping manages your response (unchangeable stressors)
Problem-focused coping is for physical stress; emotion-focused coping is for mental stress

questions.explanation: Problem-focused coping directly addresses the stressor (time management, negotiating workload, delegating, problem-solving) — most effective for changeable stressors. Emotion-focused coping manages your emotional response when you can't change the stressor (cognitive reframing, acceptance, mindfulness, seeking support) — most effective for unchangeable stressors. Adaptive coping matches strategy to stressor.

questions.question: What is behavioral activation, and why is it useful for workplace stress?

options.text: Behavioral activation means working more hours
Behavioral activation is deliberately engaging in avoided behaviors in small steps to counter stress-related avoidance and withdrawal — it helps break procrastination cycles
Behavioral activation is only for people with depression
Behavioral activation means avoiding difficult tasks

questions.explanation: Behavioral activation involves deliberately engaging in avoided behaviors in small, manageable steps. When stressed, people often avoid tasks (procrastination, withdrawal), which provides short-term relief but worsens stress long-term. Behavioral activation breaks this cycle: 'I'll work on the report for 20 minutes, then take a break.' It gathers evidence that contradicts avoidant thoughts.

questions.question: When should you use problem-focused coping vs. emotion-focused coping?

options.text: Use problem-focused coping for changeable stressors (things you can influence); emotion-focused coping for unchangeable stressors (things outside your control)

questions.explanation: Adaptive coping matches strategy to stressor controllability. Use problem-focused coping when you can change the stressor (negotiate deadline, organize workflow, ask for help). Use emotion-focused coping when you can't change it (organizational restructuring, waiting for decisions). Many workplace stressors are partially changeable — wisdom is knowing which aspects you can influence.

questions.question: What is self-compassion, and how does it relate to workplace stress?

options.text: Self-compassion means making excuses for poor performance
Self-compassion is treating yourself with kindness when struggling — acknowledging difficulty, normalizing the struggle, and offering yourself support rather than harsh self-criticism
Self-compassion is the same as self-esteem

questions.explanation: Self-compassion involves treating yourself with the same kindness you'd offer a friend in the same situation. It has three components: (1) acknowledge the difficulty ('This is genuinely hard'), (2) normalize the struggle ('Anyone would find this stressful'), (3) offer yourself kindness ('I'm doing the best I can'). It counters harsh self-criticism that amplifies workplace stress.

title: Behavioral Coping and Activation Strategies - Quiz
